mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held near the zero lower bound for an extended period lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, compressing risk premia and supporting a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 prices over a quarterly horizon as investors reach for yield.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 60-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or SPX underperforms cash in >50% of low-rate months.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month, it signals accelerating labor market deterioration that undermines consumer income expectations, causing equity markets to price in weaker forward earnings and produce negative 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns following a >0.2pp UNRATE increase are positive in more than 55% of historical triggered instances since 1990.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where INDPRO begins rising month-over-month while PAYEMS is still contracting reflects an early-cycle productivity-led recovery in which firms increase output before rehiring, a pattern historically associated with equity market troughs and forward gains as forward-looking investors price the coming labor recovery.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO=95.97 and PAYEMS=131488 as of mid-2009
failure_condition: Conditional 20-day forward SPX return during INDPRO-up/PAYEMS-down divergence periods is not statistically greater than unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05) across at least two historical recession recoveries.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-housing-collapse recovery regime, a sustained bottoming of housing starts (HOUST no longer making new lows over 3 months) signals the end of the residential investment drag on GDP, which forward-anticipating equity markets reward with positive returns over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST=582.0 as of 2009-06, near historic lows
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns show no significant positive skew (mean difference vs baseline < 0, or p > 0.10) during HOUST-stabilization episodes across post-1980 housing recessions.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive months of positive INDPRO growth reflect an ongoing manufacturing-led rebound in real economic activity, which is a leading indicator of corporate earnings improvement and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing 2-month INDPRO growth and subsequent 20-day SPX return is below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year decline in CPIAUCSL exceeding 1% signals deflationary pressure that raises real debt burdens and corporate margin risk, causing an immediate negative equity market reaction over a short 5-day horizon as investors price higher deflation-driven recession probability.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL YoY negative in mid-2009 post-crisis
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns following CPI YoY declines below -1% are positive in more than 55% of historical instances, or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly confirm a broadening consumer and housing-driven demand recovery, a combination that more reliably predicts positive S&P 500 20-day forward returns than either series alone because it rules out a single-sector rebound.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM > 0 and HOUST MoM > 0 in same month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS=342309 and HOUST=582.0 as of mid-2009
failure_condition: Joint RSAFS/HOUST positive-month signal produces 20-day forward SPX returns no better than either single-series signal alone, measured by comparative Rank IC across a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: During an early-cycle recovery regime, a deceleration in the monthly pace of PAYEMS job losses (fewer jobs lost than the prior 3-month average) signals labor market bottoming, which forward-looking equity investors reward with positive returns over a quarterly horizon as they anticipate an eventual return to net hiring.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS=131488 thousand with decelerating monthly declines by mid-2009
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns are not significantly higher (p > 0.05) during PAYEMS deceleration-of-losses periods compared to periods of accelerating job losses, across at least two historical recession recoveries.
